category_sources: Official Fabric README describes it as a Python library for executing shell commands remotely over SSH.
source_facts.description says Fabric is a library and command-line tool for SSH.
display_name_sources: source_facts.name is `fabric`; official documentation and repository use the display name Fabric.
docs_sources: Official Fabric docs navigation links to the API docs at docs.fabfile.org.
source_facts.homepage is the official Fabric documentation site.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo is empty, so repo was determined from official Fabric installing documentation, which identifies GitHub `fabric/fabric` as the canonical repository.
tags_sources: Official README states Fabric builds on Invoke and Paramiko for remote shell command execution over SSH.
source_facts.description mentions SSH and command-line use.
